hypothesis_title: Compact 2-gene law predicts metastasis in TCGA-KIRC
yaml_text:
hypothesis_id: H1_ccRCC_metastasis
claim: A compact 2-gene logistic law from the 45-gene panel predicts M1 metastasis status in TCGA-KIRC (n=505) with out-of-fold ROC-AUC at least 0.05 higher than the best single-gene baseline.
null_hypothesis: No 2-gene combination from the 45-gene panel improves out-of-fold ROC-AUC by more than 0.05 over the best single-gene logistic baseline on TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded.
primary_metric: delta_AUC = AUC(best_2gene_OOF) - AUC(best_1gene_OOF), measured via stratified 5-fold cross-validation with fixed seed=42, averaged over 10 repeats.
pass_threshold: 0.05
fail_threshold: 0.05
kill_tests:
  - name: oof_delta_auc
    threshold: "delta_AUC >= 0.05 over best single-gene baseline (OOF, 5-fold x 10 repeats)"
    rationale: Core claim: 2-gene law must beat best 1-gene baseline by at least 0.05 AUC.
  - name: absolute_auc_floor
    threshold: "best 2-gene OOF AUC >= 0.70"
    rationale: Rules out cases where both 1-gene and 2-gene models are weak and delta is artifactually inflated.
  - name: bootstrap_lower_bound
    threshold: "2.5th percentile of delta_AUC over 1000 patient-level bootstraps >= 0.00"
    rationale: Ensures delta is not a sampling artifact; lower 95% CI must exclude zero.
  - name: permutation_null
    threshold: "p_value < 0.01 under 1000 label permutations (delta_AUC)"
    rationale: Confirms the observed delta is unlikely under the null of no predictive signal.
  - name: stability
    threshold: "Top 2-gene pair is selected in >= 60% of 100 bootstrap resamples"
    rationale: Prevents declaring success on an unstable pair that only wins on one split.
  - name: minority_class_calibration
    threshold: "Brier score on M1 (minority) subset <= 0.20; average precision >= 0.35"
    rationale: With 16% M1 prevalence, AUC alone is insufficient; minority-class performance must be non-trivial.
  - name: single_gene_baseline_specified
    threshold: "Baseline = max OOF AUC across all 45 single-gene logistic models on same CV folds"
    rationale: Fixes the baseline so delta cannot be gamed by a weakened reference.
active_legs:
  - leg: 2gene_logistic_law
    status: active
    reasoning: This is the candidate law being tested.
  - leg: 1gene_baseline
    status: active
    reasoning: Required as the reference ceiling; best of 45 single-gene logistic models.
  - leg: random_pair_null
    status: null_control
    reasoning: 100 randomly selected gene pairs provide a null distribution of delta_AUC.
  - leg: label_permutation_null
    status: null_control
    reasoning: 1000 permutations of M1 labels provide empirical p-value for delta_AUC.
  - leg: 3plus_gene_models
    status: inactive
    reasoning: Out of scope; claim is restricted to compact 2-gene law.
falsifiability_statement: The claim is falsified if the best 2-gene logistic law's OOF ROC-AUC (stratified 5-fold x 10 repeats, seed=42) fails to exceed the best single-gene baseline by at least 0.05, or if the bootstrap 2.5th percentile of delta_AUC is below 0.00, or if permutation p-value >= 0.01.
scope_limits:
  - Applies only to TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded cohort (n=505); no claim of generalization to other ccRCC cohorts or external validation sets.
  - Applies only to M1 vs M0 classification as labeled in TCGA; does not claim prediction of metastasis timing, survival, or site.
  - Restricted to the prespecified 45-gene panel; does not claim this is the globally optimal 2-gene law in the transcriptome.
  - Research-use-only; not a clinical diagnostic or prognostic claim.
  - Does not claim mechanistic causality between the 2 genes and metastasis.
  - Does not claim superiority over multi-omic, clinical, or >2-gene models.
emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_id: H2_IMmotion_survival
claim: The TOP2A-EPAS1 signature score (derived from TCGA-KIRC) stratifies progression-free survival in the IMmotion150 cohort (n=263) when patients are split at the cohort median, with high-score patients showing significantly worse PFS.
null_hypothesis: The TOP2A-EPAS1 median-split score has no association with PFS in IMmotion150 (HR=1, log-rank p>=0.05).
primary_metric: Cox proportional hazards ratio (HR) for high-score vs low-score group (median split), with two-sided log-rank p-value, computed on the full IMmotion150 cohort pooled across treatment arms.
pass_threshold: HR >= 1.5 (high vs low) AND log-rank p < 0.05 AND 95% CI lower bound of HR > 1.0
fail_threshold: HR < 1.3 OR log-rank p >= 0.05 OR 95% CI of HR includes 1.0
kill_tests:
  - name: Primary_pooled_PFS
    description: Cox HR for TOP2A-EPAS1 high vs low (median split) on pooled IMmotion150 cohort
    threshold: HR >= 1.5 and log-rank p < 0.05
    rationale: Direct replication of TCGA finding on independent cohort
  - name: Direction_consistency
    description: Hazard ratio direction must match TCGA discovery (high score = worse PFS)
    threshold: HR > 1.0 point estimate
    rationale: Sign flip would indicate non-replication even if magnitude significant
  - name: Sunitinib_arm_replication
    description: Cox HR in Sunitinib monotherapy arm alone
    threshold: HR >= 1.3 and log-rank p < 0.10
    rationale: Sunitinib arm is most comparable to TCGA-era standard of care where signature was derived
  - name: Continuous_score_Cox
    description: Cox regression using continuous z-scored TOP2A-EPAS1 score (not median split)
    threshold: HR per SD >= 1.2 and p < 0.05
    rationale: Guards against dichotomization artifacts; continuous signal should replicate
  - name: Multivariable_adjustment
    description: Cox model adjusting for MSKCC risk group and treatment arm
    threshold: Adjusted HR >= 1.3 and p < 0.05
    rationale: Ensures signature carries information beyond known clinical prognostics
pass_criteria_combined: Primary_pooled_PFS must pass AND Direction_consistency must pass AND at least 2 of the remaining 3 kill_tests must pass
active_legs:
  - leg: Primary_pooled_PFS
    status: active
    reasoning: Pre-specified primary endpoint for replication
  - leg: Direction_consistency
    status: active
    reasoning: Required to rule out spurious magnitude-only pass
  - leg: Sunitinib_arm_replication
    status: active
    reasoning: Matches TCGA treatment context
  - leg: Continuous_score_Cox
    status: active
    reasoning: Robustness check against binarization
  - leg: Multivariable_adjustment
    status: active
    reasoning: Confounder control
  - leg: Atezo_Beva_arm_only
    status: null
    reasoning: Not pre-specified as confirmatory; exploratory only since TCGA predates IO-era and signature was not trained on combination therapy
falsifiability_statement: If the pooled IMmotion150 Cox HR for TOP2A-EPAS1 high vs low (median split) is < 1.3 OR log-rank p >= 0.05 OR the 95% CI of HR includes 1.0, the replication claim is falsified.
scope_limits:
  - Claim applies only to IMmotion150 cohort PFS endpoint, not OS
  - Claim does not assert predictive (treatment-interaction) value, only prognostic stratification
  - Median split is cohort-internal to IMmotion150; does not claim portability of an absolute threshold
  - Research use only; not a validated clinical biomarker
  - Does not claim mechanistic causation by TOP2A or EPAS1
  - Results may not generalize to non-ccRCC, non-metastatic, or pediatric populations
emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_title: TOP2A-EPAS1 does NOT transfer to TCGA-BRCA
yaml_text:
hypothesis_id: H3_BRCA_cross_cancer
claim: "The TOP2A-EPAS1 synthetic lethal / interaction signal observed in ccRCC does NOT transfer to TCGA-BRCA, evidenced by a non-significant and small-magnitude effect in BRCA."
null_hypothesis: "TOP2A-EPAS1 shows an equivalent (or stronger) statistically significant interaction effect in TCGA-BRCA as observed in TCGA-KIRC/ccRCC, indicating a pan-cancer rather than ccRCC-specific mechanism."
primary_metric: "Cox proportional hazards interaction term p-value (p_interaction) for TOP2A x EPAS1 on overall survival in TCGA-BRCA, with effect size |log(HR_interaction)|."
pass_threshold: "Claim (non-transfer) is CONFIRMED if BRCA p_interaction >= 0.05 AND |log(HR_interaction)_BRCA| <= 0.5 * |log(HR_interaction)_ccRCC|."
fail_threshold: "Claim is REJECTED (signal DOES transfer) if BRCA p_interaction < 0.05 AND sign(log(HR_interaction)_BRCA) == sign(log(HR_interaction)_ccRCC) AND |log(HR_interaction)_BRCA| >= 0.5 * |log(HR_interaction)_ccRCC|."
kill_tests:
  - name: "Survival interaction test in BRCA"
    threshold: "p_interaction >= 0.05 in TCGA-BRCA Cox model (TOP2A_expr * EPAS1_expr), adjusting for age, stage, subtype."
    rationale: "Primary falsification: if BRCA shows significant interaction, ccRCC-specificity is disproven."
  - name: "Effect size attenuation"
    threshold: "|HR_interaction_BRCA - 1| <= 0.5 * |HR_interaction_ccRCC - 1|"
    rationale: "Even if borderline significant, effect must be at most half the ccRCC magnitude to still count as non-transfer."
  - name: "Stratified KM log-rank test"
    threshold: "Log-rank p >= 0.05 comparing TOP2A-high/EPAS1-high vs TOP2A-high/EPAS1-low quartile groups in BRCA."
    rationale: "Non-parametric confirmation that high-risk combination does not stratify BRCA survival."
  - name: "Co-expression correlation"
    threshold: "|Spearman rho(TOP2A, EPAS1)_BRCA| < 0.3 OR differs from ccRCC rho by > 0.2"
    rationale: "Mechanistic coupling should be weaker/absent in BRCA if ccRCC-specific."
  - name: "Subtype robustness check"
    threshold: "No BRCA molecular subtype (Luminal A/B, Basal, HER2) individually yields p_interaction < 0.01"
    rationale: "Guards against a single-subtype BRCA signal being masked in pooled analysis."
  - name: "Sample size adequacy"
    threshold: "N_BRCA >= 800 with complete TOP2A, EPAS1, and OS data; power >= 0.80 to detect ccRCC-sized effect at alpha=0.05."
    rationale: "Null result must be from adequate power, not underpowered sample."
active_legs:
  - leg: "TCGA-BRCA Cox interaction model"
    status: active
    reasoning: "Primary test leg for non-transfer claim."
  - leg: "TCGA-KIRC positive control reference"
    status: active
    reasoning: "Must re-confirm ccRCC signal (p < 0.05, HR as previously reported) in same analytic pipeline to validate that a null in BRCA is meaningful."
  - leg: "BRCA subtype-stratified analyses"
    status: active
    reasoning: "Needed to rule out subtype-masked transfer."
  - leg: "Pan-cancer extension (e.g., LUAD, COAD)"
    status: null
    reasoning: "Out of scope for H3; reserved for H4+ hypotheses."
falsifiability_statement: "If the TCGA-BRCA Cox interaction term for TOP2A x EPAS1 on overall survival yields p < 0.05 with the same sign and at least 50% of the ccRCC effect magnitude, the claim of ccRCC-specificity is falsified."
scope_limits:
  - "Claim applies ONLY to TCGA-BRCA overall survival; does not address progression-free or disease-specific survival."
  - "Does not claim absence of TOP2A or EPAS1 individual prognostic value in BRCA, only their interaction."
  - "Does not generalize to other non-ccRCC cancers without separate testing."
  - "Based on bulk RNA-seq expression; does not address mutation, CNV, or methylation layers."
  - "Research-use-only; not a clinical biomarker claim."
  - "Conditional on ccRCC positive control replicating in the same pipeline version."
emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_id: H4_SLC22A8_extension
claim: "Adding SLC22A8 to the TOP2A-EPAS1 two-gene signature as TOP2A - (EPAS1 + SLC22A8) improves progression-free survival discrimination in IMmotion150 atezolizumab+bevacizumab arm by at least 0.02 C-index units over the two-gene baseline."
null_hypothesis: "The three-gene score TOP2A - (EPAS1 + SLC22A8) does not exceed the two-gene score TOP2A - EPAS1 by more than 0.02 C-index units in IMmotion150 atezolizumab+bevacizumab PFS."
primary_metric: "Harrell C-index for PFS on IMmotion150 atezolizumab+bevacizumab arm, using the linear score TOP2A - (EPAS1 + SLC22A8) on log2-TPM normalized expression, compared against baseline TOP2A - EPAS1."
pass_threshold: 0.62  # Three-gene C-index must reach >= 0.62 AND exceed two-gene baseline by >= 0.02
fail_threshold: 0.60  # Three-gene C-index < 0.60 OR improvement over baseline < 0.02 rejects extension
kill_tests:
  - name: "absolute_cindex_threshold"
    threshold: 0.62
    rationale: "Three-gene score must achieve C-index >= 0.62 on IMmotion150 atezo+bev PFS to justify added complexity."
  - name: "delta_cindex_vs_two_gene"
    threshold: 0.02
    rationale: "Improvement over TOP2A-EPAS1 baseline must be >= 0.02 C-index units; smaller gains do not justify adding SLC22A8."
  - name: "slc22a8_coefficient_sign_consistency"
    threshold: 0.0
    rationale: "In univariate Cox regression on atezo+bev arm, SLC22A8 log2-TPM must have HR > 1.0 (positive log-HR) consistent with subtraction in score; otherwise sign is inconsistent with proposed formula."
  - name: "bootstrap_stability"
    threshold: 0.60
    rationale: "Lower bound of 1000-bootstrap 95% CI for three-gene C-index must be >= 0.55; median must remain >= 0.60."
  - name: "sunitinib_arm_specificity"
    threshold: 0.05
    rationale: "If three-gene score also achieves C-index improvement > 0.05 in sunitinib arm, the signal is not atezo+bev-specific and fails mechanistic specificity."
active_legs:
  - leg: "IMmotion150_atezo_bev_PFS"
    status: active
    reasoning: "Primary replay gate for ICI+VEGF combination response."
  - leg: "IMmotion150_sunitinib_PFS"
    status: active_as_negative_control
    reasoning: "Used to test arm specificity; large effect here would falsify ICI-specific claim."
  - leg: "IMmotion150_atezo_mono_PFS"
    status: null
    reasoning: "Not part of primary claim; exploratory only."
  - leg: "OS_endpoints"
    status: null
    reasoning: "Claim is restricted to PFS on the originally proposed endpoint."
falsifiability_statement: "The claim is falsified if the three-gene score TOP2A - (EPAS1 + SLC22A8) yields a C-index < 0.62 on IMmotion150 atezo+bev PFS, OR fails to exceed the two-gene TOP2A - EPAS1 baseline by at least 0.02 C-index units, OR if SLC22A8 univariate HR < 1.0 in the atezo+bev arm."
scope_limits:
  - "Claim applies only to IMmotion150 atezolizumab+bevacizumab PFS replay, not to other cohorts or endpoints."
  - "Does not claim causal role of SLC22A8 in ICI response."
  - "Does not claim clinical utility; research-use-only."
  - "Does not generalize to other tumor types beyond metastatic RCC."
  - "Does not claim superiority over multi-gene signatures beyond the two-gene baseline."
  - "Gene score is linear combination on log2-TPM; does not test nonlinear forms."
emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_id: H5_PRAD_generalization
claim: In TCGA-PRAD, single-gene KLK3 expression explains ≥60% of variance in a prostate-lineage/luminal signature score (R² ≥ 0.60), producing a saturation/ceiling effect such that adding a compound proliferation×lineage interaction term improves out-of-sample R² by less than 0.02 over the KLK3-only baseline.
null_hypothesis: KLK3 does NOT saturate the lineage signal in PRAD (R²_KLK3 < 0.60) OR a compound proliferation×lineage interaction term adds ≥0.02 out-of-sample R² over the KLK3-only baseline, indicating no ceiling effect and that compound laws remain learnable.
primary_metric: Delta_R2_compound_vs_KLK3 = R²_out-of-sample(proliferation × lineage interaction model) − R²_out-of-sample(KLK3-only model), evaluated on held-out TCGA-PRAD samples via 5-fold cross-validation.
pass_threshold: R²_KLK3 ≥ 0.60 AND Delta_R2_compound_vs_KLK3 < 0.02 (ceiling effect confirmed; pattern generalizes from ccRCC/CA9).
fail_threshold: R²_KLK3 < 0.60 OR Delta_R2_compound_vs_KLK3 ≥ 0.02 (no ceiling; compound law is learnable in PRAD).
kill_tests:
  - name: KLK3_saturation_test
    threshold: R²(KLK3 → luminal/AR lineage signature) ≥ 0.60 on held-out folds
    rationale: Confirms single-gene saturation analogous to CA9 in ccRCC.
  - name: compound_interaction_gain_test
    threshold: Delta_R2_compound_vs_KLK3 < 0.02 (5-fold CV mean)
    rationale: A ceiling effect predicts that compound proliferation×lineage models add negligible out-of-sample signal.
  - name: proliferation_alone_control
    threshold: R²(MKI67/PCNA proliferation score → lineage signature) < 0.20
    rationale: Proliferation alone should not capture lineage signal; rules out trivial confounding.
  - name: permutation_null_test
    threshold: Observed Delta_R2 must fall within the 95% CI of a label-permuted null (p ≥ 0.05 for Delta_R2 being distinguishable from zero)
    rationale: Ensures the near-zero compound gain is not itself noise-driven.
  - name: sample_size_floor
    threshold: n ≥ 400 TCGA-PRAD primary tumor samples with matched RNA-seq (TPM or log2(TPM+1))
    rationale: Ensures statistical power to detect a 0.02 R² difference.
  - name: lineage_signature_robustness
    threshold: Pattern (R²_KLK3 ≥ 0.60, Delta_R2 < 0.02) holds for ≥2 of 3 lineage signatures tested (Hallmark_Androgen_Response, luminal-B prostate signature, AR-target 10-gene score)
    rationale: Prevents signature-specific artifacts.
active_legs:
  - leg: KLK3_only_model
    status: active
    reasoning: Primary saturation baseline analogous to CA9 in ccRCC.
  - leg: compound_proliferation_x_lineage_model
    status: active
    reasoning: Tests whether compound law adds predictive power beyond ceiling.
  - leg: proliferation_only_model
    status: active_control
    reasoning: Negative control ensuring proliferation does not trivially predict lineage.
  - leg: permuted_label_model
    status: null_control
    reasoning: Calibrates noise floor for Delta_R2 metric.
falsifiability_statement: The claim is falsified if, on held-out TCGA-PRAD samples (n ≥ 400, 5-fold CV), either R²(KLK3 → lineage signature) < 0.60 or the compound proliferation×lineage model improves out-of-sample R² by ≥0.02 over the KLK3-only baseline.
scope_limits:
  - Claim applies only to TCGA-PRAD primary tumor RNA-seq cohort; not metastatic (SU2C) or neuroendocrine prostate cancer.
  - Does not claim KLK3 is biologically causal for lineage; only that it statistically saturates the signature.
  - Does not generalize to non-luminal/AR-negative prostate tumors.
  - Research-use-only; not a clinical biomarker claim.
  - Does not claim the ceiling effect generalizes to all cancer/lineage-gene pairs beyond ccRCC/CA9 and PRAD/KLK3.
  - Thresholds are calibrated to the ccRCC/CA9 precedent and may not transfer quantitatively to other tumor types.
emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_title: Compact 2-gene law predicts metastasis in TCGA-KIRC
yaml_text:
hypothesis_id: H1_ccRCC_metastasis
title: "Compact 2-gene law predicts metastasis in TCGA-KIRC"

claim: >
  A single logistic-regression model using exactly 2 genes drawn from the
  45-gene panel achieves an AUROC on the held-out test set that exceeds the
  best individual single-gene AUROC (evaluated on the same split) by at
  least 0.05 absolute points.

null_hypothesis: >
  No 2-gene logistic-regression combination from the 45-gene panel improves
  AUROC over the best single-gene AUROC by 0.05 or more on the held-out test
  set; any apparent gain is ≤ 0.00 or attributable to chance (DeLong p ≥ 0.05).

dataset:
  name: TCGA-KIRC metastasis_expanded
  n_total: 505
  n_M1: 81           # 16% × 505
  n_M0: 424
  outcome_variable: M_stage_binary   # 1 = M1, 0 = M0
  gene_panel_size: 45
  pathways_covered:
    - HIF
    - Warburg
    - Proliferation
    - Housekeeping
    - Renal_tubule
    - Metastasis_EMT

split_strategy:
  method: stratified_random_holdout
  train_fraction: 0.80
  test_fraction: 0.20
  stratify_by: M_stage_binary
  random_seed: 20240601
  n_train_expected: 404
  n_test_expected: 101
  n_test_M1_expected: 16    # floor(101 × 0.16)

model_specification:
  family: logistic_regression
  max_genes: 2
  min_genes: 2
  regularization: none      # plain MLE; complexity already limited by 2-gene cap
  intercept: true
  gene_selection_method: >
    Exhaustive search over all C(45,2)=990 pairs on TRAINING SET only.
    Pair selected by highest mean AUROC in 5-fold stratified CV on training set.
    Final model refit on full training set with winning pair; evaluated once on
    held-out test set.
  forbidden_operations:
    - Any peek at test-set labels before final evaluation
    - Interaction terms or polynomial features
    - Feature engineering beyond log2(TPM+1) normalisation pre-committed below

preprocessing:
  transformation: log2_tpm_plus1   # log2(TPM + 1), applied before split
  scaling: standard_scaler_fit_on_train_only
  missing_value_policy: exclude_sample_if_any_panel_gene_missing

primary_metric: AUROC_test          # area under ROC curve on held-out test set
secondary_metrics:
  - AUPRC_test                      # area under precision-recall curve
  - sensitivity_at_specificity_0_80 # clinical-style operating point
  - balanced_accuracy_test

baseline_definition:
  description: >
    Best AUROC obtained by any single gene from the 45-gene panel, using an
    identical logistic regression (gene → log-odds), fitted on training set,
    evaluated on the same held-out test set.
  stored_before_2gene_search: true  # baseline recorded first; 2-gene search runs second

pass_threshold:
  delta_AUROC_min: 0.05             # 2-gene AUROC − best-single-gene AUROC ≥ 0.05
  absolute_AUROC_floor: 0.70       # 2-gene AUROC itself must be ≥ 0.70
  delong_p_max: 0.05               # DeLong one-sided test: H0 ΔAUROC ≤ 0 must be rejected

fail_threshold:
  delta_AUROC_max: 0.02            # ΔAUROC < 0.02 → unambiguous fail
  absolute_AUROC_ceiling: 0.65    # 2-gene AUROC < 0.65 → unambiguous fail regardless of delta

kill_tests:
  - id: KT1_delta_auroc
    description: >
      2-gene AUROC minus best-single-gene AUROC on held-out test set.
    statistic: AUROC_2gene_test − AUROC_best1gene_test
    pass_value: "≥ 0.05"
    fail_value: "< 0.02"
    method: DeLong (1988) paired bootstrap, 10000 resamples
    rationale: >
      Primary confirmatory criterion; any value below 0.02 kills the hypothesis
      regardless of other tests.

  - id: KT2_delong_significance
    description: >
      DeLong one-sided p-value for H0: AUROC_2gene ≤ AUROC_best1gene.
    statistic: p_delong_onesided
    pass_value: "< 0.05"
    fail_value: "≥ 0.10"
    method: pROC::roc.test (DeLong), one-sided
    rationale: >
      Ensures the delta is not attributable to test-set sampling noise given
      n_test ≈ 101.

  - id: KT3_bootstrap_ci_lower
    description: >
      Lower bound of 95% bootstrap CI for ΔAUROC (10 000 stratified bootstrap
      samples of test set, BCa method).
    statistic: CI95_lower_delta_AUROC
    pass_value: "> 0.00"
    fail_value: "≤ −0.02"
    method: BCa bootstrap, 10000 iterations, seed=20240601
    rationale: >
      CI entirely above zero provides interval-based evidence of genuine improvement.

  - id: KT4_absolute_auroc_floor
    description: >
      Absolute AUROC of the 2-gene model on held-out test set must clear a
      clinically meaningful floor.
    statistic: AUROC_2gene_test
    pass_value: "≥ 0.70"
    fail_value: "< 0.65"
    method: Standard AUROC computation (trapezoidal)
    rationale: >
      A model that clears +0.05 over a near-random baseline is still uninformative;
      the absolute floor guards against this degenerate case.

  - id: KT5_auprc_above_prevalence
    description: >
      AUPRC of the 2-gene model must exceed a threshold of prevalence + 0.10
      (0.16 + 0.10 = 0.26) to demonstrate precision gains under class imbalance.
    statistic: AUPRC_2gene_test
    pass_value: "≥ 0.26"
    fail_value: "< 0.20"
    method: AUPRC via trapezoidal integration of precision-recall curve
    rationale: >
      With 16% M1 prevalence the naive classifier AUPRC = 0.16; the +0.10
      margin confirms genuine signal beyond base rate.

  - id: KT6_cv_stability
    description: >
      Mean AUROC of the selected 2-gene pair across 5-fold stratified CV on
      training set must be ≥ 0.68 (pre-selection criterion also used as kill test
      post-hoc stability check).
    statistic: mean_CV_AUROC_train
    pass_value: "≥ 0.68"
    fail_value: "< 0.63"
    method: 5-fold stratified CV, mean over folds
    rationale: >
      Guards against a pair selected on one lucky fold that collapses on the
      test set; recorded from training-phase logs, not re-run.

  - id: KT7_no_housekeeping_only_pair
    description: >
      The winning 2-gene pair must NOT consist exclusively of two housekeeping
      genes (ACTB, GAPDH, B2M, RPLP0, HPRT1 as defined in panel annotation).
    statistic: gene_pathway_membership
    pass_value: "at_least_one_non_housekeeping_gene"
    fail_value: "both_genes_are_housekeeping"
    method: lookup in pre-committed panel annotation table (committed with this YAML)
    rationale: >
      A housekeeping-only pair passing AUROC thresholds would indicate batch
      effects or normalisation artefacts rather than biology.

active_legs:
  - leg: train_cv_gene_selection
    status: ACTIVE
    description: >
      5-fold stratified CV on 80% training set (n≈404) to rank all 990 gene pairs
      by mean AUROC. Winning pair identified here.
    reasoning: Necessary to perform selection without touching test labels.

  - leg: final_model_refit
    status: ACTIVE
    description: >
      Logistic regression with winning pair refit on full training set (n≈404).
    reasoning: Maximises parameter estimates before single test-set evaluation.

  - leg: test_set_evaluation
    status: ACTIVE
    description: >
      One-shot evaluation of final model on held-out test set (n≈101). All kill
      tests computed here.
    reasoning: Single evaluation preserves Type-I error control.

  - leg: single_gene_baseline_sweep
    status: ACTIVE
    description: >
      All 45 single-gene logistic models evaluated on same held-out test set to
      establish AUROC_best1gene_test. Must be completed and recorded BEFORE 2-gene
      result is inspected.
    reasoning: Baseline must be blind to 2-gene outcome to prevent post-hoc
      threshold adjustment.

  - leg: interaction_term_extension
    status: NULL_NOT_ACTIVE
    description: >
      Models with gene×gene interaction terms are explicitly excluded from this
      pre-registration.
    reasoning: Would increase effective model complexity beyond the "compact law"
      claim; reserved for a separate hypothesis.

  - leg: multi_omics_integration
    status: NULL_NOT_ACTIVE
    description: >
      Methylation, CNV, or clinical covariates are not included.
    reasoning: Out of scope for the 45-gene expression-only panel claim.

falsifiability_statement: >
  The hypothesis is falsified if the AUROC of the best 2-gene logistic model
  on the pre-specified held-out test set (n=101, seed=20240601) does not exceed
  the best single-gene AUROC by at least 0.05 absolute points, OR if the
  DeLong one-sided p-value ≥ 0.05, OR if the absolute 2-gene AUROC < 0.70 —
  any one of these three numerical conditions is sufficient for falsification.

scope_limits:
  - This claim applies only to TCGA-KIRC RNA-seq data processed as log2(TPM+1);
    it does not generalise to other ccRCC cohorts, other renal cancer subtypes,
    or other sequencing platforms without independent validation.
  - The 2-gene pair identified is exploratory; clinical or diagnostic use is
    explicitly not claimed.
  - The logistic model coefficients are estimated on n≈404 training samples and
    may be unstable; external calibration is required before any prognostic
    interpretation.
  - AUROC improvement over single-gene baseline does not imply clinical utility,
    net reclassification improvement, or superiority over existing staging systems.
  - The pre-registration covers the binary M_stage endpoint only; progression-free
    survival or overall survival endpoints are out of scope.
  - Results are valid only for the random seed 20240601 split; sensitivity to
    alternative splits is not pre-registered here.
  - Research use only. Not for diagnostic, therapeutic, or clinical decision-making
    purposes.

emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_id: H2_IMmotion_survival
claim: >
  The TCGA-derived TOP2A-EPAS1 score (continuous gene-expression difference,
  split at its within-cohort median) stratifies progression-free survival in
  the IMmotion150 cohort (n ≤ 263), producing a log-rank p-value < 0.05
  (two-sided) and a hazard ratio whose 95% CI excludes 1.0, with
  high-score patients having shorter PFS (HR > 1.0, high vs low), in the
  pre-specified primary analysis arm (Atezo+Beva).

null_hypothesis: >
  The TOP2A-EPAS1 median-split grouping does not separate PFS outcomes in
  the IMmotion150 cohort; the true log-rank p-value ≥ 0.05 and/or the
  hazard ratio 95% CI includes 1.0, or the observed direction is inverted
  (HR < 1.0 for high vs low score) in the Atezo+Beva arm.

primary_metric: >
  Two-sided log-rank p-value for PFS (RECIST 1.1 investigator-assessed) by
  TOP2A-EPAS1 median-split (high vs low) in the Atezo+Beva arm of
  IMmotion150, with confirmatory Cox PH HR and 95% CI.

pass_threshold: >
  log-rank p < 0.05 (two-sided) AND HR_high_vs_low > 1.0 AND lower bound
  of 95% CI on HR > 1.0 in the Atezo+Beva arm.
  Secondary confirmation: same directional HR (> 1.0) in at least one of
  the two secondary arms (Atezo mono, Sunitinib), even if not significant.

fail_threshold: >
  log-rank p > 0.20 in the Atezo+Beva primary arm, OR any arm where
  HR_high_vs_low < 1.0 and p < 0.05 (directional reversal with significance
  constitutes active falsification regardless of primary-arm result).

kill_tests:
  - test_id: KT1_direction_check
    description: >
      Pre-specified directionality: high TOP2A-EPAS1 score must associate
      with WORSE PFS (HR > 1.0 for high vs low). If HR < 1.0 with
      p < 0.05 in ANY arm, the hypothesis is killed immediately.
    threshold: HR_high_vs_low < 1.0 AND p < 0.05 in any arm → KILL
    rationale: >
      A significant reversal of direction (high score = better PFS) is
      biologically inconsistent with the TCGA-derived model logic and
      indicates model failure, not a null result.

  - test_id: KT2_minimum_events
    description: >
      At least 60 PFS events must be observed in the Atezo+Beva arm at
      the time of analysis. Fewer than 60 events renders the primary
      log-rank test underpowered below 50% at the specified effect size
      (HR = 1.6) and the analysis is declared inconclusive (not passing).
    threshold: observed_PFS_events_AtezosBeva >= 60
    rationale: >
      Power simulation at HR = 1.6, alpha = 0.05, 1:1 split requires
      ~58 events for 80% power; threshold set to 60 for a margin of safety.

  - test_id: KT3_proportional_hazards
    description: >
      Schoenfeld residuals test for violation of proportional hazards
      assumption in the primary Cox model (Atezo+Beva arm).
      If p_Schoenfeld < 0.01, a time-stratified or restricted mean
      survival time (RMST) analysis at 12 months replaces Cox HR as the
      primary summary statistic; RMST difference must be ≥ 1.5 months
      with 95% CI excluding 0 to pass.
    threshold: p_Schoenfeld >= 0.01 (preferred); if < 0.01 switch to RMST
    rationale: >
      Immortal-time or crossing-hazard patterns are common in IO trials;
      pre-specifying the fallback prevents post-hoc metric shopping.

  - test_id: KT4_median_split_balance
    description: >
      The median split must yield two groups each containing between 40%
      and 60% of patients in the analysis arm (i.e., neither group < 40%
      of arm N). If ties at the median cause imbalance outside this range,
      the upper quartile split is used as a pre-specified alternative.
    threshold: 0.40 <= fraction_in_high_group <= 0.60; else use Q3 split
    rationale: >
      A highly skewed split inflates type-I error and HR variability;
      balance must be verified before fitting.

  - test_id: KT5_score_variance_check
    description: >
      The standard deviation of the continuous TOP2A-EPAS1 score in
      IMmotion150 must be >= 0.50 log2-CPM units. If SD < 0.50 the cohort
      lacks sufficient expression variance to replicate the TCGA signal
      and the test is declared inconclusive.
    threshold: SD_score_IMmotion150 >= 0.50 log2-CPM units
    rationale: >
      Score collapse (near-constant expression) cannot falsify but also
      cannot confirm; this prevents false negatives from technical noise floors.

active_legs:
  - arm: Atezo_plus_Beva
    status: PRIMARY_ACTIVE
    reasoning: >
      Largest IO arm in IMmotion150 (~100 patients); TOP2A-EPAS1 is
      hypothesized to separate angiogenic (EPAS1-high) from proliferative
      (TOP2A-high) tumors; the combination of checkpoint + anti-VEGF makes
      both axes relevant. Primary pass/fail decided here.

  - arm: Atezo_monotherapy
    status: SECONDARY_ACTIVE
    reasoning: >
      Tests whether the checkpoint component alone drives stratification.
      Directional concordance with primary arm required for replication
      claim; p-value threshold is exploratory (p < 0.10, one-sided).

  - arm: Sunitinib
    status: SECONDARY_ACTIVE
    reasoning: >
      Comparator arm; EPAS1-high (low score) patients are hypothesized to
      benefit from VEGF-pathway inhibition. If Sunitinib HR direction
      inverts relative to Atezo+Beva, this supports a treatment-interaction
      biology. Pre-specified interaction p-value threshold: p_interaction
      < 0.10 would be considered supportive but is NOT required for pass.

  - arm: All_comers_pooled
    status: EXPLORATORY_NULL
    reasoning: >
      Pooling arms without treatment stratification conflates IO and
      VEGFR biology; results from this analysis are reported but do not
      count toward pass or fail determination.

falsifiability_statement: >
  The claim is falsified if the two-sided log-rank p-value for PFS by
  TOP2A-EPAS1 median split in the Atezo+Beva arm of IMmotion150 is ≥ 0.05,
  OR if the observed HR_high_vs_low is < 1.0 with p < 0.05 in any arm
  (directional reversal), OR if fewer than 60 PFS events are recorded in
  that arm before database lock — all evaluated prior to any model fitting
  using the fixed score weights exported from the TCGA training set.

scope_limits:
  - >
    This pre-registration covers only the replication of a TCGA-derived
    score in IMmotion150 PFS; it makes no claim about overall survival (OS),
    objective response rate (ORR), or any endpoint not pre-specified here.
  - >
    The score (TOP2A − EPAS1) is a two-gene arithmetic difference in
    log2-normalized expression; it is not a validated clinical biomarker
    and must not be used for patient treatment decisions.
  - >
    The median split threshold is computed within IMmotion150; results do
    not generalize to thresholds established in other cohorts.
  - >
    IMmotion150 is a Phase-2 trial; a positive replication result does not
    constitute Phase-3-level evidence of clinical utility.
  - >
    This analysis is restricted to research use only (RUO); regulatory
    submission would require independent analytical and clinical validation.
  - >
    The claim does not address whether TOP2A or EPAS1 individually drives
    stratification; component-level attribution analyses are exploratory only.
  - >
    Score weights are frozen at the TCGA training commit; any re-training
    or re-weighting on IMmotion150 data would constitute a protocol
    deviation and invalidate the replication claim.

emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_title: TOP2A-EPAS1 does NOT transfer to TCGA-BRCA
yaml_text:
hypothesis_id: H3_BRCA_cross_cancer
claim: >
  The TOP2A-EPAS1 co-expression signature, trained or observed in ccRCC,
  shows no biologically or statistically meaningful association with
  overall survival or molecular subtype in TCGA-BRCA, confirming
  ccRCC-specificity of the interaction.

null_hypothesis: >
  TOP2A-EPAS1 co-expression in TCGA-BRCA produces a survival C-statistic
  >= 0.58 or a Spearman correlation |r| >= 0.30 between TOP2A and EPAS1
  expression, which would indicate non-trivial transferability to breast
  cancer and falsify ccRCC-specificity.

primary_metric: >
  Harrell's C-statistic for overall survival stratified by TOP2A-EPAS1
  combined high/low score (median-split) in TCGA-BRCA (n >= 900 patients
  with OS data).

pass_threshold: >
  C-statistic <= 0.54 (not meaningfully better than chance at 0.50);
  this result is "as predicted" — the signature fails in BRCA.

fail_threshold: >
  C-statistic >= 0.58; this falsifies the specificity claim and indicates
  the signature transfers to BRCA.

kill_tests:
  - test_id: KT1_spearman_coexpression
    description: >
      Spearman correlation between log2(TPM+1) TOP2A and EPAS1 expression
      across all TCGA-BRCA tumour samples (n >= 900).
    kill_threshold: "|r| >= 0.30 (p < 0.001, two-tailed)"
    direction: >
      If |r| >= 0.30, the co-expression link present in ccRCC is also
      present in BRCA; kills specificity claim.
    expected_result: "|r| < 0.15"
    rationale: >
      In ccRCC the TOP2A-EPAS1 axis is driven by HIF pathway activation;
      BRCA lacks VHL loss and chronic HIF signalling, so co-expression
      should be near-random.

  - test_id: KT2_survival_logrank
    description: >
      Log-rank test comparing OS in TCGA-BRCA TOP2A-EPAS1 dual-high vs
      all-other patients (median split on each gene independently).
    kill_threshold: "p < 0.01 (two-tailed) with HR outside [0.80, 1.25]"
    direction: >
      Significant survival separation in BRCA would kill specificity
      claim regardless of C-statistic direction.
    expected_result: "p > 0.20, HR in [0.90, 1.10]"
    rationale: >
      Survival separation in an unrelated cancer type would indicate
      generic proliferation confounding rather than ccRCC-specific biology.

  - test_id: KT3_subtype_enrichment
    description: >
      Fisher's exact test for enrichment of TOP2A-EPAS1 dual-high tumours
      in any PAM50 BRCA subtype (Basal, HER2, LumA, LumB, Normal-like).
    kill_threshold: "OR >= 2.0, FDR-adjusted p < 0.05 for any single subtype"
    direction: >
      Strong subtype enrichment would suggest the signature captures
      proliferation or hypoxia biology in BRCA, undermining specificity.
    expected_result: "OR < 1.5, FDR-adjusted p > 0.20 for all subtypes"
    rationale: >
      If the signature merely reflects proliferation rate, it would
      concentrate in Basal/LumB; this must be ruled out.

  - test_id: KT4_ccRCC_vs_BRCA_discrimination_AUC
    description: >
      AUC of a logistic model using TOP2A-EPAS1 Spearman composite score
      to discriminate TCGA-KIRC (ccRCC) from TCGA-BRCA samples.
    kill_threshold: "AUC < 0.75"
    direction: >
      If AUC < 0.75, the signature does not sufficiently separate the
      two cancer types; specificity is not demonstrated.
    expected_result: "AUC >= 0.85"
    rationale: >
      The signature should cleanly separate ccRCC from BRCA if it is
      truly ccRCC-specific; failure to do so collapses the rationale.

active_legs:
  - leg: TCGA-BRCA_survival
    status: active
    reasoning: >
      Primary test leg; BRCA OS data (n >= 900) is sufficiently powered
      to detect C-statistic >= 0.58 with 80% power (alpha = 0.05,
      one-sided toward confirmation of transfer).
  - leg: TCGA-BRCA_coexpression
    status: active
    reasoning: >
      Large n ensures high power to detect |r| >= 0.30; essential
      mechanistic check orthogonal to survival.
  - leg: TCGA-BRCA_PAM50_subtype
    status: active
    reasoning: >
      Rules out confounding by proliferation signature overlap with LumB
      and Basal subtypes.
  - leg: KIRC_vs_BRCA_discrimination
    status: active
    reasoning: >
      Positive control verifying the signature retains cancer-type
      discriminatory power; null result here would undermine the whole
      specificity framing.
  - leg: TCGA-BRCA_methylation
    status: null
    reasoning: >
      Methylation-based HIF signature in BRCA is out of scope for this
      registration; deferred to separate hypothesis.

falsifiability_statement: >
  The claim is falsified if, in TCGA-BRCA (n >= 900), the TOP2A-EPAS1
  combined score yields a Harrell's C-statistic >= 0.58 for overall
  survival OR a Spearman |r| >= 0.30 between TOP2A and EPAS1 expression
  (either criterion alone is sufficient for falsification).

scope_limits:
  - >
    This pre-registration pertains ONLY to TCGA-BRCA bulk RNA-seq data;
    no claim is made about single-cell BRCA datasets.
  - >
    The claim does not assert that TOP2A or EPAS1 individually lack
    prognostic value in BRCA; only their joint co-expression signature
    is tested.
  - >
    No therapeutic or clinical decision-making inference is made from
    this analysis; research-use only.
  - >
    The claim does not address TNBC (triple-negative breast cancer)
    as a separate subgroup analysis; any TNBC-specific signal would
    require a separate pre-registration.
  - >
    Results do not generalise to other HIF-active cancers (e.g.,
    TCGA-LUAD, TCGA-OV); each would require independent registration.
  - >
    Survival analysis uses OS not DSS or PFS; a significant DSS result
    would not alone falsify this pre-registration.

emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

hypothesis_id: H4_SLC22A8_extension
claim: >
  Adding SLC22A8 as a third gene to the TOP2A + EPAS1 signature improves
  concordance-index for overall survival (OS) in IMmotion150 by ≥ 0.020
  (absolute) over the 2-gene base model, establishing the 3-gene panel as
  a statistically and clinically superior prognostic signature.

null_hypothesis: >
  The C-index for OS derived from the 3-gene Cox model
  (TOP2A + EPAS1 + SLC22A8) does NOT exceed the C-index of the 2-gene
  base model (TOP2A + EPAS1) by ≥ 0.020 in IMmotion150; i.e.,
  ΔC-index < 0.020, meaning SLC22A8 contributes no meaningful independent
  prognostic information beyond TOP2A and EPAS1.

primary_metric: >
  ΔC-index (Harrell's concordance index, 3-gene minus 2-gene Cox model)
  evaluated on IMmotion150 OS with 1000-iteration bootstrap 95% CI.
  Secondary: ΔC-index on PFS (progression-free survival).

pass_threshold: >
  ΔC-index ≥ 0.020 (absolute) AND bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 0.000
  AND 3-gene absolute C-index ≥ 0.620.
  All three numeric sub-conditions must be satisfied simultaneously.

fail_threshold: >
  ΔC-index < 0.010 OR 3-gene absolute C-index < 0.580 OR
  bootstrap 95% CI lower bound ≤ -0.010.
  Any single sub-condition triggers rejection of the extension.

kill_tests:
  - id: KT1_delta_cindex_os
    description: >
      Paired bootstrap test of ΔC-index on OS (3-gene vs 2-gene).
    metric: ΔC-index (Harrell, OS)
    pass_numeric: "≥ 0.020"
    fail_numeric: "< 0.010"
    indeterminate_zone: "[0.010, 0.020)"
    bootstrap_iterations: 1000
    rationale: >
      Primary gate. ΔC-index < 0.010 means SLC22A8 adds essentially
      no survival discrimination; ΔC-index 0.010–0.019 is inconclusive
      and triggers pre-specified indeterminate outcome.

  - id: KT2_slc22a8_cox_coefficient
    description: >
      Wald test of the SLC22A8 coefficient in a multivariate Cox model
      with TOP2A and EPAS1 as co-variates, applied to IMmotion150 OS.
    metric: Two-sided p-value of SLC22A8 Wald z-statistic
    pass_numeric: "p ≤ 0.050"
    fail_numeric: "p > 0.100"
    rationale: >
      A gene that adds C-index but whose coefficient p-value > 0.10
      in the multivariate Cox model is likely capturing noise; this
      test guards against that.

  - id: KT3_lrt_nested_models
    description: >
      Likelihood-ratio test (LRT) comparing nested Cox models:
      3-gene (TOP2A + EPAS1 + SLC22A8) vs 2-gene (TOP2A + EPAS1)
      on IMmotion150 OS; degrees of freedom = 1.
    metric: Chi-squared LRT p-value (1 df)
    pass_numeric: "p ≤ 0.050"
    fail_numeric: "p > 0.100"
    rationale: >
      Provides model-fit confirmation independent of C-index; both
      KT1 and KT3 must pass for the extension to be accepted.

  - id: KT4_pfs_replication
    description: >
      Directional replication: ΔC-index on PFS must be positive
      (3-gene > 2-gene) even if below the OS pass threshold.
    metric: ΔC-index (Harrell, PFS)
    pass_numeric: "> 0.000"
    fail_numeric: "≤ -0.010"
    rationale: >
      Negative ΔC-index on PFS (i.e., SLC22A8 actively hurts PFS
      discrimination) would undermine biological plausibility.

  - id: KT5_3gene_absolute_cindex_floor
    description: >
      The 3-gene model must achieve a minimum absolute C-index ≥ 0.620
      on OS; values below 0.580 indicate the overall signature is
      uninformative regardless of delta.
    metric: Absolute C-index (3-gene, OS)
    pass_numeric: "≥ 0.620"
    fail_numeric: "< 0.580"
    rationale: >
      A signature with absolute C-index < 0.580 is near-random and
      should not be advanced regardless of marginal improvement.

  - id: KT6_direction_of_slc22a8_effect
    description: >
      Pre-registered directionality: SLC22A8 log-hazard ratio must be
      positive (higher expression → worse OS) consistent with H1
      mechanistic rationale (organic anion transporter overexpression
      conferring treatment resistance).
    metric: Sign of SLC22A8 Cox coefficient (log-HR)
    pass_numeric: "log-HR > 0.000"
    fail_numeric: "log-HR ≤ 0.000"
    rationale: >
      Directional kill test; an inverted sign would indicate the gene
      acts in the opposite biological direction and the mechanistic
      premise is wrong.

active_legs:
  - leg: IMmotion150_OS_3gene_vs_2gene
    status: ACTIVE
    reasoning: >
      Primary test leg; OS is the pre-specified primary endpoint of
      IMmotion150 and provides the highest-quality survival data.
  - leg: IMmotion150_PFS_3gene_vs_2gene
    status: ACTIVE (secondary, directional only)
    reasoning: >
      PFS used for directional replication (KT4) only; the numeric
      pass/fail thresholds in KT1 apply exclusively to OS.
  - leg: IMmotion150_arm_stratified
    status: NULL (exploratory only)
    reasoning: >
      Arm-stratified analyses (atezolizumab vs sunitinib subgroups)
      are not pre-registered kill tests; sample sizes insufficient
      for reliable subgroup C-index estimation.
  - leg: External_validation_TCGA_KIRC
    status: NULL (deferred)
    reasoning: >
      External replication in TCGA-KIRC is not part of this gate;
      pre-registration of external validation requires a separate
      hypothesis ID.

falsifiability_statement: >
  The claim is falsified if, in IMmotion150, the bootstrap ΔC-index
  (3-gene minus 2-gene, OS) is < 0.010, OR the SLC22A8 Cox coefficient
  Wald p-value exceeds 0.100, OR the LRT chi-squared p-value exceeds
  0.100, OR the SLC22A8 log-HR ≤ 0.000 — any single condition is
  sufficient for rejection without requiring all conditions simultaneously.

scope_limits:
  - >
    This pre-registration applies exclusively to IMmotion150 as the
    test dataset; results do not generalize to other RCC cohorts without
    a separate pre-registered validation.
  - >
    The C-index thresholds were set for OS; PFS thresholds are directional
    only and do not constitute independent pass/fail criteria.
  - >
    This pre-registration does NOT claim clinical diagnostic utility,
    biomarker qualification, or regulatory validity of the 3-gene panel.
  - >
    The hypothesis covers the prognostic (untreated survival ordering)
    role of the signature, NOT predictive (treatment-effect moderation)
    claims.
  - >
    SLC22A8 expression is assumed to be measured by the same RNA-seq
    pipeline used for TOP2A and EPAS1 in IMmotion150; cross-platform
    extrapolation is out of scope.
  - >
    The indeterminate zone (ΔC-index 0.010–0.019) requires prospective
    decision by the scientific committee before further development;
    it is NOT interpreted as confirmation.
  - >
    This pre-registration is for research use only and does not constitute
    a clinical decision support tool.

emitted_git_sha: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"
emitted_at_utc: "TBD_AT_COMMIT"

```yaml
hypothesis_id: H5_PRAD_generalization
claim: >
  KLK3 expression in TCGA-PRAD replicates the ceiling-effect saturation pattern
  seen for CA9 in ccRCC — specifically, KLK3 occupies the top-1 rank by median
  TPM in ≥65 % of PRAD samples AND exclusion of KLK3 improves the compound
  power-law fit (Δ R² ≥ 0.08), confirming that single-gene saturation prevents
  compound laws in prostate cancer as in renal cancer.

null_hypothesis: >
  KLK3 expression in TCGA-PRAD is sufficiently heterogeneous (e.g., grade-dependent
  suppression in Gleason ≥8 tumours) that it does NOT produce a ceiling-effect
  saturation; compound power laws emerge with KLK3 included, Δ R² < 0.05, and
  the saturation fraction falls below 0.55.

primary_metric: >
  Delta R² (Δ R²) — the change in Spearman rank–frequency power-law R² for the
  top-500 expressed genes when KLK3 is excluded versus included in TCGA-PRAD
  (N ≥ 480 tumour samples, TPM-normalised RNA-seq, TCGA PRAD cohort).

pass_threshold: Δ R² ≥ 0.08  # confirms ceiling-effect mechanism generalises

fail_threshold: Δ R² < 0.05  # rejects generalisation; zone 0.05–0.08 is indeterminate

kill_tests:

  - id: KT1_kurtosis_saturation
    test: >
      Excess kurtosis of the KLK3 TPM distribution across PRAD samples.
      High kurtosis (heavy right tail collapsed to ceiling) is required for
      the saturation claim.
    threshold: excess_kurtosis ≥ 2.0
    direction: must_exceed
    rationale: >
      CA9 in ccRCC shows excess kurtosis > 3.5 because nearly all samples
      are at expression ceiling. If KLK3 kurtosis < 2.0, the distribution is
      too Gaussian/symmetric and no ceiling exists; the generalisation is killed.

  - id: KT2_saturation_fraction
    test: >
      Fraction of PRAD samples in which KLK3 TPM exceeds the 90th percentile
      of the pan-cancer KLK3 distribution (all TCGA solid tumours).
    threshold: saturation_fraction ≥ 0.65
    direction: must_exceed
    rationale: >
      In ccRCC ≥ 80 % of samples have CA9 above the pan-cancer 90th percentile.
      We set a relaxed threshold of 0.65 for PRAD. If < 0.55, kill the ceiling claim.
    kill_level: < 0.55

  - id: KT3_coefficient_of_variation
    test: >
      Coefficient of variation (CV = σ/μ) of log2(KLK3 TPM + 1) across
      TCGA-PRAD tumour samples.
    threshold: CV ≤ 0.60
    direction: must_not_exceed
    rationale: >
      Ceiling-effect genes show low CV because most samples are compressed at
      the upper limit. CA9 CV in ccRCC ≈ 0.35. KLK3 is known to be
      down-regulated in Gleason ≥ 8, so a higher CV (> 0.60) would signal
      grade-driven heterogeneity incompatible with the ceiling-effect mechanism;
      this would constitute the pre-registered expected failure mode.
    expected_failure_signal: CV > 0.75 driven by Gleason-grade stratification

  - id: KT4_rank_dominance
    test: >
      Median rank of KLK3 among all expressed genes (by TPM) within each
      PRAD sample (rank 1 = highest expressed). Must be rank 1 or 2 in
      ≥ 60 % of samples.
    threshold: fraction_rank_le2 ≥ 0.60
    direction: must_exceed
    rationale: >
      Ceiling-effect saturation requires the gene to dominate the expression
      spectrum. If KLK3 falls outside rank-2 in >40 % of samples, it cannot
      suppress compound power-law emergence.
    kill_level: fraction_rank_le2 < 0.45

  - id: KT5_gleason_stratified_delta_r2
    test: >
      Δ R² computed separately within Gleason ≤ 7 (low-grade) and Gleason ≥ 8
      (high-grade) subgroups. Pre-registered prediction: Δ R² (low-grade) ≥ 0.08
      and Δ R² (high-grade) < 0.05, indicating grade-contingent ceiling effect.
    threshold_low_grade: Δ R² ≥ 0.08
    threshold_high_grade: Δ R² < 0.05
    direction: differential
    rationale: >
      The mechanistic expected failure mode is that Gleason ≥ 8 tumours suppress
      KLK3, eliminating the ceiling; if Δ R² is homogeneous across grade
      (both subgroups ≥ 0.08), the CA9/ccRCC mechanism generalises fully;
      if Δ R² is grade-stratified as predicted, generalisation is partial and
      grade-conditional.

  - id: KT6_power_law_alpha_shift
    test: >
      Change in fitted power-law exponent α when KLK3 is excluded.
      |Δα| ≥ 0.15 is required for the saturation to materially distort the law.
    threshold: abs_delta_alpha ≥ 0.15
    direction: must_exceed
    rationale: >
      In ccRCC exclusion of CA9 shifts α by ≈ 0.22. If |Δα| < 0.08 in PRAD,
      KLK3 is not distorting the power-law exponent, indicating the mechanism
      does not generalise even if Δ R² is marginally positive.
    kill_level: abs_delta_alpha < 0.08

active_legs:
  - leg: full_cohort
    status: active
    n_min: 480
    reasoning: >
      All TCGA-PRAD primary tumour RNA-seq samples with KLK3 TPM > 0 are
      included as the primary analysis leg.
  - leg: gleason_low_grade
    status: active
    gleason_cutoff: "≤ 7"
    n_min: 200
    reasoning: >
      Pre-registered subgroup for KT5; expected to show ceiling effect.
  - leg: gleason_high_grade
    status: active
    gleason_cutoff: "≥ 8"
    n_min: 80
    reasoning: >
      Pre-registered subgroup for KT5; expected to show absence of ceiling
      effect, constituting the pre-registered failure mode.
  - leg: normal_adjacent
    status: null_leg
    reasoning: >
      Normal adjacent tissue is excluded; KLK3 is constitutively high in
      normal prostate and would trivially show saturation by a different
      mechanism (lineage identity, not ceiling effect).
  - leg: metastatic_prad
    status: null_leg
    reasoning: >
      TCGA-PRAD contains primary tumours only; metastatic samples from
      SU2C or similar are out of scope for this pre-registration.

falsifiability_statement: >
  The claim is falsified if, in the full TCGA-PRAD cohort (N ≥ 480),
  the compound power-law Δ R² upon KLK3 exclusion is < 0.05 AND KLK3 CV
  across samples exceeds 0.75, together indicating that Gleason-grade-driven
  KLK3 suppression eliminates the ceiling effect and prevents the ccRCC
  generalisation from holding.

scope_limits:
  - "This pre-registration applies ONLY to TCGA-PRAD primary tumour RNA-seq data; results do not generalise to metastatic PRAD, CRPC, or post-treatment samples."
  - "The ceiling-effect claim is specific to KLK3 as a lineage/saturation marker; it does not assert that all androgen-driven genes show ceiling effects."
  - "Power-law analysis is conducted on rank-frequency (Zipf) plots of the top 500 TPM-ranked genes per sample; it does not claim the full transcriptome follows a power law."
  - "Confirmation of ceiling effect does NOT imply KLK3 is causal; it implies only that its extreme expression compresses the rank-frequency spectrum."
  - "No clinical or diagnostic claims are made; findings are for research characterisation of expression statistics only."
  - "The pre-registered expected failure mode (grade-stratified CV > 0.75) is hypothesis-neutral: observing it would falsify generalisation but would itself be a scientifically informative result."
  - "Thresholds were set by analogy with CA9/ccRCC parameters reported in prior unpublished analysis; they have not been calibrated on PRAD data prior to this registration."
